lines: | ✅ | [#761](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/761) **Incident objects (G-d phase 3)** — `incidents` table anchored to `(asset_id, check_id)` (migration `c4e5a6b7d8f9`; lifecycle `open→acknowledged→resolved`, occurrence attach, reopen chain via `prior_incident_id`, **partial unique index** = at most one active incident per pair) + `incident_service` lifecycle engine (`INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` dedup, upsert-race-safe per #420 — proven by a genuine two-connection concurrent race test) + `incident_evidence` deterministic layer-1 card (metric trend, upstream pipeline delay-vs-history, sibling checks, downstream blast radius — **never `sample_failures` content**, PII-safe) + fail-soft `run_suite` hook (before alert dispatch) + `RunReport.incidents` carries the card on the `ResultPublisher` payload + per-suite `auto_resolve_incidents` toggle (default on) + `GET/POST /api/v1/incidents{,/ack,/resolve}` with grant-derived authz (read=view, act=edit, 404-no-leak, admin sees all) + **Incidents section on AssetDetail** (edit-gated ack/resolve). Full incidents page defers to #773 nav inversion | G-d / Theme 2 |

| 🟡 | [#773](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/773) **Navigation inversion — assets become the primary lens (G-d phase 4, deliberately last)** — presentation-only inversion, zero authz change (everything renders from the same #760 grant-derived reads). Dashboard now **leads with an asset-health strip** (`AssetHealthLead` — Monitored / Need-attention / In-progress tiles + a needs-attention list, all from the existing `/assets` read; click-through to `/assets` and each asset); the KPI/trends stay below. Sidebar reordered so **Assets sits first after Dashboard, above Suites** (suites secondary). **Suite detail + run detail surface an "Asset" link chip** (`AssetLink`) that navigates to `/assets/{id}` (consuming the `asset_id` already on `SuiteRead`/`RunRead` since #760; hidden when null). **Admin-only owner-reassignment** on AssetDetail (`OwnerBlock` — user picker sourced from the admin-only `GET /admin/users`, resolves the owner to a display name/email not a bare UUID; the existing admin-gated `PATCH /assets/{id}` `owner_user_id` branch was already shipped + tested in #772, so backend is unchanged). Mobile pass per #688/#693 (stacked tiles, `dq-suite-row` hover, `wrap`). Frontend-only PR | G-d |

| ✅ | [#801](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/801) **Mobile overlay nav** (user follow-up to #617/#688) — below the `lg` breakpoint the Sider collapses to zero width and the nav moves into an overlay `Drawer` (AntD's default scrim; closes on nav-click, scrim, or Esc) that floats **above** the content, so the page keeps its full width whether the nav is open or shut; desktop is unchanged. The two nav menus are extracted into a shared `SideNav` used by both the desktop Sider and the Drawer (single DOM copy per breakpoint). Detection mechanism (Sider `onBreakpoint`) untouched, so jsdom stays desktop and the existing shell tests are unaffected. Tests: `App.test` narrow-mode block (matchMedia-driven — hamburger + Drawer overlay + close-on-nav) + Playwright `mobile-nav.spec.ts` at 390px (content width unchanged open-vs-shut, scrim closes without navigating) — [#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817) | UI follow-up |

+| ✅ | [#802](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/802) **Hierarchical asset browse** (user follow-up to #760/#773) — the Assets page gains a **By source** lens (now default) that drills **datasource → database/catalog → schema → table**, derived purely from each asset's ADR 0034 OpenLineage `namespace` + `name` (namespace = the connection-agnostic datasource root; `name` splits on `.` for warehouses / `/` for flat-file paths). Tree derivation is a pure, unit-tested module (`assetTree.ts` — namespace grouping, prefix-merge, slash/dot split, folder-and-leaf collision) kept out of the `.tsx`. **No backend change** (assets are connection-agnostic by ADR 0034). Tests: `assetTree.test` (pure) + `Assets.test` (tree default, leaf-open, folder-no-nav, table toggle) + `assets.spec` e2e (tree drill + table lens + ≥2-suite detail) — [#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818) | UI follow-up |

| ✅ | [#767](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/767) **Per-check results misattributed when a check errors (P1, found by the 2026-07-10 live schema-drift test)** — GX 1.17 returns errored expectations first; the positional zip in `run_service` then cross-wired results to the wrong `check_id` (both SF + UC, deterministic). Fixed in the GX translation layer ([PR #771](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/771)): `dataq_index` meta-marker stamped per expectation, `to_suite_outcome` re-keys by it (duplicate-safe; warns on partial marker loss; non-dict legacy `meta` passes through to GX's own error). Red/green regression tests incl. a real missing-column error on the pandas path. The same live session verified **schema-drift detection 6/6** (add/remove/type-change × SF/UC via GX guards) and the **#759 add/drop-view lineage propagation E2E** against live prod DBs; UX finding filed [#768](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/768), sweep follow-up [#770](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/770) | G-d / Theme 6 |

| ✅ | **All-datasource perf baseline captured 2026-07-10** (unscheduled, last-cloud-window work): Snowflake pushdown green to **200M rows** (worker flat); full-load ceilings on a 2 Gi worker — CSV 2M→5M, parquet 5M→10M, UC 1M→2M, Iceberg 2M→5M (prod replica OOM). Doc renamed [perf-baseline.md](perf-baseline.md) + published to the docs site. ## What & why

Fixes #802. The Assets page was a single flat table, one row per object. Add a **By source** lens (now the default) that drills **datasource → database/catalog → schema → table**, with the flat table retained as the secondary **All assets** lens.

This is a **presentation regrouping**, not a new identity: the tree is derived purely from each asset's ADR 0034 OpenLineage `namespace` + `name`. The namespace is the connection-agnostic datasource root; the `name` splits on `.` (warehouses / UC / Iceberg) or `/` (flat-file paths). **No backend change** — assets are connection-agnostic by ADR 0034, so grouping by namespace is the honest "connection root".

## Changes
- `components/assets/assetTree.ts` (new, pure): `buildAssetTree` (namespace grouping, prefix-merge, folder-and-leaf collision), `datasourceKind`, `nameSegments`, `expandableKeys`.
- `pages/Assets.tsx`: `Segmented` "By source" / "All assets" toggle; AntD `Tree` with datasource-kind icons, per-leaf env + health tags; leaves open the existing AssetDetail, folders just expand. Flat table unchanged under "All assets".

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] Tree/drill-down browse rooted at connections (namespace); leaves open AssetDetail
- [x] Env grouping preserved (per-leaf env tag; distinct-namespace assets stay separate roots)
- [x] Flat table retained as a secondary "All assets" list/search view

## Testing
- `assetTree.test.ts` (pure): scheme classification, dot/slash split, prefix-merge, folder-and-leaf collision, expandable keys.
- `Assets.test.tsx`: tree default (namespace root + drill levels + leaf health), leaf-opens-detail, folder-does-not-navigate, "All assets" table toggle + row nav, multi-datasource grouping.
- `e2e/assets.spec.ts`: tree drill → detail, "All assets" table lens, ≥2-suite shared-asset detail — all green against the live local stack.
- Full local gate: tsc, eslint, prettier, 499 unit tests, coverage gate (all-files 89.3% lines; Assets.tsx 100%).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

)" 2>&1 | tail -2

gh api repos/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pulls/818/comments -f commit_id="$SHA" -f path="frontend/src/pages/Assets.tsx" -F line=121 -f side=RIGHT -f body="**[low · simplification]** \`key={defaultExpandedKeys.length}\` remounts the whole \`Tree\` whenever the *count* of expandable nodes changes, purely to re-seed \`defaultExpandedKeys\`. In practice \`AssetsTree\` already remounts on the view toggle and mounts with data present, so the only case this covers is an in-place data reload — where it would also silently wipe any nodes the user had manually collapsed. Two structurally-different trees with the same expandable-count also won't re-seed.

Minor and harmless given how rarely the asset list reloads in place. If you keep it, consider keying on something stable-yet-representative (e.g. the root count or a cheap structural hash) or dropping it entirely — \`defaultExpandedKeys\` on first mount is already correct. Not blocking." 2>&1 | grep -E '"html_url"|message' | head -2

content.content:
1	"""Read-only asset view — the browse/reason surface over `assets` (ADR 0034, #760).
2	
3	Assets are what users *reason about*; suites remain how checks *execute* (ADR 0034
4	guiding principle). This module aggregates, per asset, the suites that target it +
5	their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood, for the `/assets` API.
6	
7	**Authz is derived, never granted (ADR 0034 decision 5 / ADR 0027).** An asset is
8	visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 suite mapped to it (`suites.asset_id`); the
9	aggregation is filtered to *only* the suites the caller's grants cover; a
10	workspace-admin sees every suite (`include_all`). An asset wholly outside the
11	caller's grants is 404-no-leak (the API layer raises `AssetNotFoundError`). This
12	reuses `suite_service.accessible_suite_ids` verbatim, so the visibility rule has a
13	single source of truth and can never drift from the suites/runs surfaces.
14	
15	Asset-metadata mutation (owner, description) is workspace-Admin-only — enforced at
16	the API layer (`require_workspace_admin`), not here; `update_asset_metadata` is the
17	plain persistence half.
18	
19	Lineage nodes are **not** authz-filtered: blast radius is the point (ADR 0034 §2),
20	and a node exposes only its OpenLineage `(namespace, name)` + whether it is
21	monitored — never run data or grants — so nothing sensitive leaks through it.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import uuid
27	from collections import defaultdict
28	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
29	from datetime import datetime
30	
31	from sqlalchemy import select
32	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
33	
34	from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
35	from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
36	from backend.app.db.models import Asset, Run, Suite, User, worst_severity
37	from backend.app.lineage.edges import downstream_assets, upstream_assets
38	from backend.app.services.run_service import check_outcome_counts
39	from backend.app.services.suite_authz import effective_permissions
40	from backend.app.services.suite_service import accessible_suite_ids
41	
42	log = get_logger(__name__)
43	
44	
45	class AssetNotFoundError(DataQError):
46	    """Raised when an asset does not exist *or* is wholly outside the caller's
47	    grants — the two are indistinguishable by design (404-no-leak, ADR 0027)."""
48	
49	    status_code = 404
50	    code = "asset_not_found"
51	
52	
53	class AssetOwnerInvalidError(DataQError):
54	    """The `owner_user_id` on a metadata update names no existing user — checked
55	    up front (the share-grant FK-precheck idiom, `share_service.grant_share`) so a
56	    bad id is a clean 422, never a raw FK IntegrityError surfacing as 500."""
57	
58	    status_code = 422
59	    code = "asset_owner_invalid"
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True)
63	class RunOutcome:
64	    """A suite's latest run outcome — execution status + the data-quality summary.
65	
66	    ``worst_severity`` is the highest failing tier among evaluated checks (warn <
67	    fail < critical), or ``None`` when all passed / nothing evaluated. ``run_id`` /
68	    timestamps are ``None`` when the suite has never run."""
69	
70	    run_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
71	    status: str | None = None
72	    worst_severity: str | None = None
73	    checks_total: int = 0
74	    checks_passed: int = 0
75	    finished_at: datetime | None = None
76	    created_at: datetime | None = None
77	
78	
79	@dataclass(frozen=True)
80	class ComposingSuite:
81	    """One suite the caller can see that targets the asset, with its latest run."""
82	
83	    suite_id: uuid.UUID
84	    name: str
85	    my_permission: str
86	    latest_run: RunOutcome
87	
88	
89	@dataclass(frozen=True)
90	class AssetSummary:
91	    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset."""
92	
93	    id: uuid.UUID
94	    namespace: str
95	    name: str
96	    env: str | None
97	    description: str | None
98	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
99	    last_seen: datetime
100	    suite_count: int
101	    # Health rolled up across the caller-visible composing suites' latest runs.
102	    worst_severity: str | None
103	    checks_total: int
104	    checks_passed: int
105	    last_run_at: datetime | None
106	    # Latest-run *execution* states (distinct from check severity — a run can fail
107	    # operationally and write no results, so severity alone would read green):
108	    # any latest run `failed` / any latest run still `queued`/`running`. The UI
109	    # health badge consumes these so an operational failure is never shown passing.
110	    has_failed_run: bool = False
111	    has_active_run: bool = False
112	
113	
114	@dataclass(frozen=True)
115	class LineageNode:
116	    """A lineage neighbour — enough to render, no run data (ADR 0034 §2)."""
117	
118	    id: uuid.UUID
119	    namespace: str
120	    name: str
121	    env: str | None
122	    is_monitored: bool
123	
124	
125	@dataclass(frozen=True)
126	class AssetDetail:
127	    """Asset detail: the summary aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage."""
128	
129	    summary: AssetSummary
130	    suites: list[ComposingSuite]
131	    upstream: list[LineageNode] = field(default_factory=list)
132	    downstream: list[LineageNode] = field(default_factory=list)
133	
134	
135	# ── internals ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136	
137	
138	def _latest_run_per_suite(session: Session, suite_ids: list[uuid.UUID]) -> dict[uuid.UUID, Run]:
139	    """The most-recent run for each suite (DISTINCT ON, newest `created_at`)."""
140	    if not suite_ids:
141	        return {}
142	    rows = session.scalars(
143	        select(Run)
144	        .where(Run.suite_id.in_(suite_ids))
145	        .distinct(Run.suite_id)
146	        .order_by(Run.suite_id, Run.created_at.desc())
147	    )
148	    return {run.suite_id: run for run in rows}
149	
150	
151	def _run_outcome(run: Run | None, outcome: tuple[int, int, str | None] | None) -> RunOutcome:
152	    """Assemble a `RunOutcome` from a suite's latest run + its check-outcome tuple."""
153	    if run is None:
154	        return RunOutcome()
155	    total, passed, worst = outcome or (0, 0, None)
156	    return RunOutcome(
157	        run_id=run.id,
158	        status=run.status,
159	        worst_severity=worst,
160	        checks_total=total,
161	        checks_passed=passed,
162	        finished_at=run.finished_at,
163	        created_at=run.created_at,
164	    )
165	
166	
167	def _composing_suites(
168	    suites: list[Suite],
169	    levels: dict[uuid.UUID, str | None],
170	    latest_runs: dict[uuid.UUID, Run],
171	    outcomes: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]],
172	) -> list[ComposingSuite]:
173	    """Build the per-suite breakdown for one asset's suites (sorted by name)."""
174	    composing: list[ComposingSuite] = []
175	    for suite in suites:
176	        level = levels.get(suite.id)
177	        if level is None:  # defensive: only reachable suites are passed in
178	            continue
179	        run = latest_runs.get(suite.id)
180	        outcome = outcomes.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
181	        composing.append(
182	            ComposingSuite(
183	                suite_id=suite.id,
184	                name=suite.name,
185	                my_permission=level,
186	                latest_run=_run_outcome(run, outcome),
187	            )
188	        )
189	    return composing
190	
191	
192	def _roll_up(asset: Asset, composing: list[ComposingSuite]) -> AssetSummary:
193	    """Roll a set of composing suites up into the asset-level health summary."""
194	    statuses: list[str] = []
195	    checks_total = checks_passed = 0
196	    last_run_at: datetime | None = None
197	    has_failed_run = has_active_run = False
198	    for suite in composing:
199	        run = suite.latest_run
200	        if run.worst_severity is not None:
201	            statuses.append(run.worst_severity)
202	        # Execution state, distinct from check severity (see AssetSummary): a
203	        # `failed` run wrote no results and must not roll up green; an active run
204	        # hasn't concluded yet.
205	        if run.status == "failed":
206	            has_failed_run = True
207	        elif run.status in ("queued", "running"):
208	            has_active_run = True
209	        checks_total += run.checks_total
210	        checks_passed += run.checks_passed
211	        ts = run.finished_at or run.created_at
212	        if ts is not None and (last_run_at is None or ts > last_run_at):
213	            last_run_at = ts
214	    return AssetSummary(
215	        id=asset.id,
216	        namespace=asset.namespace,
217	        name=asset.name,
218	        env=asset.env,
219	        description=asset.description,
220	        owner_user_id=asset.owner_user_id,
221	        last_seen=asset.last_seen,
222	        suite_count=len(composing),
223	        worst_severity=worst_severity(statuses),
224	        checks_total=checks_total,
225	        checks_passed=checks_passed,
226	        last_run_at=last_run_at,
227	        has_failed_run=has_failed_run,
228	        has_active_run=has_active_run,
229	    )
230	
231	
232	# ── public API ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
233	
234	
235	def list_visible_assets(
236	    session: Session,
237	    *,
238	    user_id: uuid.UUID,
239	    include_all: bool = False,
240	    limit: int = 200,
241	    offset: int = 0,
242	) -> list[AssetSummary]:
243	    """Assets the caller can see (via ≥1 accessible composing suite), sorted by
244	    ``(namespace, name)`` and paginated with ``limit``/``offset``.
245	
246	    Pagination is applied at the SQL level over the *asset* page (not the suite
247	    rows), so a page is a stable, deterministic slice regardless of how many
248	    suites compose each asset. Aggregation is filtered to the caller's grants: a
249	    partial-grant caller sees the asset but only the suites/runs they can view
250	    roll up into its health."""
251	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
252	    visible_asset_ids = select(Suite.asset_id).where(
253	        Suite.asset_id.is_not(None), Suite.id.in_(accessible)
254	    )
255	    assets = list(
256	        session.scalars(
257	            select(Asset)
258	            .where(Asset.id.in_(visible_asset_ids))
259	            .order_by(Asset.namespace, Asset.name)
260	            .limit(limit)
261	            .offset(offset)
262	        )
263	    )
264	    if not assets:
265	        return []
266	
267	    suites = list(
268	        session.scalars(
269	            select(Suite)
270	            .where(Suite.asset_id.in_([a.id for a in assets]), Suite.id.in_(accessible))
271	            .order_by(Suite.name)
272	        )
273	    )
274	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
275	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
276	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()])
277	
278	    suites_by_asset: dict[uuid.UUID, list[Suite]] = defaultdict(list)
279	    for suite in suites:
280	        assert suite.asset_id is not None  # filtered in the query
281	        suites_by_asset[suite.asset_id].append(suite)
282	
283	    return [
284	        _roll_up(
285	            asset,
286	            _composing_suites(suites_by_asset.get(asset.id, []), levels, latest_runs, outcomes),
287	        )
288	        for asset in assets
289	    ]
290	
291	
292	def get_visible_asset(
293	    session: Session, asset_id: uuid.UUID, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
294	) -> AssetDetail:
295	    """One asset's detail (aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage).
296	
297	    Raises `AssetNotFoundError` (404) if the asset does not exist or the caller can
298	    view no suite targeting it — the two are indistinguishable (no-leak). A
299	    workspace-admin (``include_all``) sees every asset, **including suite-less
300	    orphans** (e.g. a lineage-only node, or an asset whose last composing suite was
301	    deleted) — for them only a truly unknown id 404s."""
302	    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
303	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
304	    suites = list(
305	        session.scalars(
306	            select(Suite)
307	            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset_id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
308	            .order_by(Suite.name)
309	        )
310	    )
311	    # No-leak: for a non-admin, an unknown id and an id they can see no suite for
312	    # both 404. An admin's visibility is workspace-wide (ADR 0027), so a suite-less
313	    # asset is still returned (with an empty suites list) rather than hidden.
314	    if asset is None or (not suites and not include_all):
315	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
316	
317	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
318	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
319	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()])
320	    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes)
321	
322	    summary = _roll_up(asset, composing)
323	    upstream = _lineage_nodes(session, upstream_assets(session, asset_id))
324	    downstream = _lineage_nodes(session, downstream_assets(session, asset_id))
325	    return AssetDetail(summary=summary, suites=composing, upstream=upstream, downstream=downstream)
326	
327	
328	def summarize_asset(
329	    session: Session, asset: Asset, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
330	) -> AssetSummary:
331	    """Roll one already-loaded asset up into its list-row summary.
332	
333	    Unlike `get_visible_asset`, this never 404s on "no composing suites" — an asset
334	    with zero suites rolls up to an empty (no-run) health summary. Used by the
335	    admin PATCH response, where the asset need not have suites to have metadata."""
336	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
337	    suites = list(
338	        session.scalars(
339	            select(Suite)
340	            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset.id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
341	            .order_by(Suite.name)
342	        )
343	    )
344	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id) if suites else {}
345	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
346	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()])
347	    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes)
348	    return _roll_up(asset, composing)
349	
350	
351	def _lineage_nodes(session: Session, assets: list[Asset]) -> list[LineageNode]:
352	    """Map reachable lineage assets to render-only nodes (+ monitored flag).
353	
354	    ``is_monitored`` = the asset has ≥1 suite targeting it (globally — a structural
355	    fact, not a grant), resolved for all nodes in one grouped query (no N+1)."""
356	    if not assets:
357	        return []
358	    ids = [a.id for a in assets]
359	    monitored = {
360	        asset_id
361	        for (asset_id,) in session.execute(
362	            select(Suite.asset_id).where(Suite.asset_id.in_(ids)).group_by(Suite.asset_id)
363	        )
364	    }
365	    return [
366	        LineageNode(
367	            id=a.id,
368	            namespace=a.namespace,
369	            name=a.name,
370	            env=a.env,
371	            is_monitored=a.id in monitored,
372	        )
373	        for a in assets
374	    ]
375	
376	
377	def update_asset_metadata(
378	    session: Session,
379	    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
380	    *,
381	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
382	    description: str | None = None,
383	    set_owner: bool = False,
384	    set_description: bool = False,
385	) -> Asset:
386	    """Set an asset's owner and/or description (workspace-Admin-only; gated at API).
387	
388	    ``set_owner`` / ``set_description`` are the partial-update discriminators, so a
389	    field can be explicitly cleared to ``None`` versus left untouched (mirrors the
390	    suite PATCH's None-means-leave-alone problem, made explicit). Raises
391	    `AssetNotFoundError` (404) for an unknown id — no authz derivation here, since a
392	    workspace-admin sees every asset (ADR 0027) — and `AssetOwnerInvalidError` (422)
393	    when ``owner_user_id`` names no existing user (FK pre-check, never a raw 500)."""
394	    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
395	    if asset is None:
396	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
397	    if set_owner:
398	        if owner_user_id is not None and session.get(User, owner_user_id) is None:
399	            raise AssetOwnerInvalidError(
400	                "owner user does not exist", detail={"owner_user_id": str(owner_user_id)}
401	            )
402	        asset.owner_user_id = owner_user_id
403	    if set_description:
404	        asset.description = description
405	    session.commit()
406	    session.refresh(asset)
407	    log.info("asset_metadata_updated", asset_id=str(asset.id))
408	    return asset
409

file.content:
"""Read-only asset view — the browse/reason surface over `assets` (ADR 0034, #760).

Assets are what users *reason about*; suites remain how checks *execute* (ADR 0034
guiding principle). This module aggregates, per asset, the suites that target it +
their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood, for the `/assets` API.

**Authz is derived, never granted (ADR 0034 decision 5 / ADR 0027).** An asset is
visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 suite mapped to it (`suites.asset_id`); the
aggregation is filtered to *only* the suites the caller's grants cover; a
workspace-admin sees every suite (`include_all`). An asset wholly outside the
caller's grants is 404-no-leak (the API layer raises `AssetNotFoundError`). This
reuses `suite_service.accessible_suite_ids` verbatim, so the visibility rule has a
single source of truth and can never drift from the suites/runs surfaces.

Asset-metadata mutation (owner, description) is workspace-Admin-only — enforced at
the API layer (`require_workspace_admin`), not here; `update_asset_metadata` is the
plain persistence half.

Lineage nodes are **not** authz-filtered: blast radius is the point (ADR 0034 §2),
and a node exposes only its OpenLineage `(namespace, name)` + whether it is
monitored — never run data or grants — so nothing sensitive leaks through it.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from collections import defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
from backend.app.db.models import Asset, Run, Suite, User, worst_severity
from backend.app.lineage.edges import downstream_assets, upstream_assets
from backend.app.services.run_service import check_outcome_counts
from backend.app.services.suite_authz import effective_permissions
from backend.app.services.suite_service import accessible_suite_ids

log = get_logger(__name__)


class AssetNotFoundError(DataQError):
    """Raised when an asset does not exist *or* is wholly outside the caller's
    grants — the two are indistinguishable by design (404-no-leak, ADR 0027)."""

    status_code = 404
    code = "asset_not_found"


class AssetOwnerInvalidError(DataQError):
    """The `owner_user_id` on a metadata update names no existing user — checked
    up front (the share-grant FK-precheck idiom, `share_service.grant_share`) so a
    bad id is a clean 422, never a raw FK IntegrityError surfacing as 500."""

    status_code = 422
    code = "asset_owner_invalid"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunOutcome:
    """A suite's latest run outcome — execution status + the data-quality summary.

    ``worst_severity`` is the highest failing tier among evaluated checks (warn <
    fail < critical), or ``None`` when all passed / nothing evaluated. ``run_id`` /
    timestamps are ``None`` when the suite has never run."""

    run_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
    status: str | None = None
    worst_severity: str | None = None
    checks_total: int = 0
    checks_passed: int = 0
    finished_at: datetime | None = None
    created_at: datetime | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ComposingSuite:
    """One suite the caller can see that targets the asset, with its latest run."""

    suite_id: uuid.UUID
    name: str
    my_permission: str
    latest_run: RunOutcome


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AssetSummary:
    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset."""

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    name: str
    env: str | None
    description: str | None
    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
    last_seen: datetime
    suite_count: int
    # Health rolled up across the caller-visible composing suites' latest runs.
    worst_severity: str | None
    checks_total: int
    checks_passed: int
    last_run_at: datetime | None
    # Latest-run *execution* states (distinct from check severity — a run can fail
    # operationally and write no results, so severity alone would read green):
    # any latest run `failed` / any latest run still `queued`/`running`. The UI
    # health badge consumes these so an operational failure is never shown passing.
    has_failed_run: bool = False
    has_active_run: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class LineageNode:
    """A lineage neighbour — enough to render, no run data (ADR 0034 §2)."""

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    name: str
    env: str | None
    is_monitored: bool


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AssetDetail:
    """Asset detail: the summary aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage."""

    summary: AssetSummary
    suites: list[ComposingSuite]
    upstream: list[LineageNode] = field(default_factory=list)
    downstream: list[LineageNode] = field(default_factory=list)


# ── internals ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _latest_run_per_suite(session: Session, suite_ids: list[uuid.UUID]) -> dict[uuid.UUID, Run]:
    """The most-recent run for each suite (DISTINCT ON, newest `created_at`)."""
    if not suite_ids:
        return {}
    rows = session.scalars(
        select(Run)
        .where(Run.suite_id.in_(suite_ids))
        .distinct(Run.suite_id)
        .order_by(Run.suite_id, Run.created_at.desc())
    )
    return {run.suite_id: run for run in rows}


def _run_outcome(run: Run | None, outcome: tuple[int, int, str | None] | None) -> RunOutcome:
    """Assemble a `RunOutcome` from a suite's latest run + its check-outcome tuple."""
    if run is None:
        return RunOutcome()
    total, passed, worst = outcome or (0, 0, None)
    return RunOutcome(
        run_id=run.id,
        status=run.status,
        worst_severity=worst,
        checks_total=total,
        checks_passed=passed,
        finished_at=run.finished_at,
        created_at=run.created_at,
    )


def _composing_suites(
    suites: list[Suite],
    levels: dict[uuid.UUID, str | None],
    latest_runs: dict[uuid.UUID, Run],
    outcomes: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]],
) -> list[ComposingSuite]:
    """Build the per-suite breakdown for one asset's suites (sorted by name)."""
    composing: list[ComposingSuite] = []
    for suite in suites:
        level = levels.get(suite.id)
        if level is None:  # defensive: only reachable suites are passed in
            continue
        run = latest_runs.get(suite.id)
        outcome = outcomes.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
        composing.append(
            ComposingSuite(
                suite_id=suite.id,
                name=suite.name,
                my_permission=level,
                latest_run=_run_outcome(run, outcome),
            )
        )
    return composing


def _roll_up(asset: Asset, composing: list[ComposingSuite]) -> AssetSummary:
    """Roll a set of composing suites up into the asset-level health summary."""
    statuses: list[str] = []
    checks_total = checks_passed = 0
    last_run_at: datetime | None = None
    has_failed_run = has_active_run = False
    for suite in composing:
        run = suite.latest_run
        if run.worst_severity is not None:
            statuses.append(run.worst_severity)
        # Execution state, distinct from check severity (see AssetSummary): a
        # `failed` run wrote no results and must not roll up green; an active run
        # hasn't concluded yet.
        if run.status == "failed":
            has_failed_run = True
        elif run.status in ("queued", "running"):
            has_active_run = True
        checks_total += run.checks_total
        checks_passed += run.checks_passed
        ts = run.finished_at or run.created_at
        if ts is not None and (last_run_at is None or ts > last_run_at):
            last_run_at = ts
    return AssetSummary(
        id=asset.id,
        namespace=asset.namespace,
        name=asset.name,
        env=asset.env,
        description=asset.description,
        owner_user_id=asset.owner_user_id,
        last_seen=asset.last_seen,
        suite_count=len(composing),
        worst_severity=worst_severity(statuses),
        checks_total=checks_total,
        checks_passed=checks_passed,
        last_run_at=last_run_at,
        has_failed_run=has_failed_run,
        has_active_run=has_active_run,
    )


# ── public API ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def list_visible_assets(
    session: Session,
    *,
    user_id: uuid.UUID,
    include_all: bool = False,
    limit: int = 200,
    offset: int = 0,
) -> list[AssetSummary]:
    """Assets the caller can see (via ≥1 accessible composing suite), sorted by
    ``(namespace, name)`` and paginated with ``limit``/``offset``.

    Pagination is applied at the SQL level over the *asset* page (not the suite
    rows), so a page is a stable, deterministic slice regardless of how many
    suites compose each asset. Aggregation is filtered to the caller's grants: a
    partial-grant caller sees the asset but only the suites/runs they can view
    roll up into its health."""
    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
    visible_asset_ids = select(Suite.asset_id).where(
        Suite.asset_id.is_not(None), Suite.id.in_(accessible)
    )
    assets = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Asset)
            .where(Asset.id.in_(visible_asset_ids))
            .order_by(Asset.namespace, Asset.name)
            .limit(limit)
            .offset(offset)
        )
    )
    if not assets:
        return []

    suites = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Suite)
            .where(Suite.asset_id.in_([a.id for a in assets]), Suite.id.in_(accessible))
            .order_by(Suite.name)
        )
    )
    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()])

    suites_by_asset: dict[uuid.UUID, list[Suite]] = defaultdict(list)
    for suite in suites:
        assert suite.asset_id is not None  # filtered in the query
        suites_by_asset[suite.asset_id].append(suite)

    return [
        _roll_up(
            asset,
            _composing_suites(suites_by_asset.get(asset.id, []), levels, latest_runs, outcomes),
        )
        for asset in assets
    ]


def get_visible_asset(
    session: Session, asset_id: uuid.UUID, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
) -> AssetDetail:
    """One asset's detail (aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage).

    Raises `AssetNotFoundError` (404) if the asset does not exist or the caller can
    view no suite targeting it — the two are indistinguishable (no-leak). A
    workspace-admin (``include_all``) sees every asset, **including suite-less
    orphans** (e.g. a lineage-only node, or an asset whose last composing suite was
    deleted) — for them only a truly unknown id 404s."""
    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
    suites = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Suite)
            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset_id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
            .order_by(Suite.name)
        )
    )
    # No-leak: for a non-admin, an unknown id and an id they can see no suite for
    # both 404. An admin's visibility is workspace-wide (ADR 0027), so a suite-less
    # asset is still returned (with an empty suites list) rather than hidden.
    if asset is None or (not suites and not include_all):
        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})

    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()])
    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes)

    summary = _roll_up(asset, composing)
    upstream = _lineage_nodes(session, upstream_assets(session, asset_id))
    downstream = _lineage_nodes(session, downstream_assets(session, asset_id))
    return AssetDetail(summary=summary, suites=composing, upstream=upstream, downstream=downstream)


def summarize_asset(
    session: Session, asset: Asset, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
) -> AssetSummary:
    """Roll one already-loaded asset up into its list-row summary.

    Unlike `get_visible_asset`, this never 404s on "no composing suites" — an asset
    with zero suites rolls up to an empty (no-run) health summary. Used by the
    admin PATCH response, where the asset need not have suites to have metadata."""
    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
    suites = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Suite)
            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset.id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
            .order_by(Suite.name)
        )
    )
    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id) if suites else {}
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()])
    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes)
    return _roll_up(asset, composing)


def _lineage_nodes(session: Session, assets: list[Asset]) -> list[LineageNode]:
    """Map reachable lineage assets to render-only nodes (+ monitored flag).

    ``is_monitored`` = the asset has ≥1 suite targeting it (globally — a structural
    fact, not a grant), resolved for all nodes in one grouped query (no N+1)."""
    if not assets:
        return []
    ids = [a.id for a in assets]
    monitored = {
        asset_id
        for (asset_id,) in session.execute(
            select(Suite.asset_id).where(Suite.asset_id.in_(ids)).group_by(Suite.asset_id)
        )
    }
    return [
        LineageNode(
            id=a.id,
            namespace=a.namespace,
            name=a.name,
            env=a.env,
            is_monitored=a.id in monitored,
        )
        for a in assets
    ]


def update_asset_metadata(
    session: Session,
    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
    *,
    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    set_owner: bool = False,
    set_description: bool = False,
) -> Asset:
    """Set an asset's owner and/or description (workspace-Admin-only; gated at API).

    ``set_owner`` / ``set_description`` are the partial-update discriminators, so a
    field can be explicitly cleared to ``None`` versus left untouched (mirrors the
    suite PATCH's None-means-leave-alone problem, made explicit). Raises
    `AssetNotFoundError` (404) for an unknown id — no authz derivation here, since a
    workspace-admin sees every asset (ADR 0027) — and `AssetOwnerInvalidError` (422)
    when ``owner_user_id`` names no existing user (FK pre-check, never a raw 500)."""
    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
    if asset is None:
        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
    if set_owner:
        if owner_user_id is not None and session.get(User, owner_user_id) is None:
            raise AssetOwnerInvalidError(
                "owner user does not exist", detail={"owner_user_id": str(owner_user_id)}
            )
        asset.owner_user_id = owner_user_id
    if set_description:
        asset.description = description
    session.commit()
    session.refresh(asset)
    log.info("asset_metadata_updated", asset_id=str(asset.id))
    return asset

=== check_outcome_counts ===
341:def check_outcome_counts(
def check_outcome_counts(
    session: Session, run_ids: Sequence[uuid.UUID]
) -> dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]]:
    """Per-run ``(checks_total, checks_passed, worst_severity)`` for a set of runs,
    in a single grouped query (no N+1). ``worst_severity`` is the highest of
    warn/fail/critical present, else ``None`` (all passed / only operational).

    ``checks_total``/``checks_passed`` count **evaluated** checks — the four
    severity tiers (pass/warn/fail/critical) — and **exclude** operational
    ``skip``/``error`` (#122), so the X/Y matches the run-detail page's "Checks
    passed" denominator and an all-skip run reports total 0 (rendered ``—``, not a
    misleading green ``0/N``).

    Lets the runs list surface a run's *data-quality* outcome — distinct from the
    run's *execution* status, which is ``succeeded`` even when checks failed."""
    if not run_ids:
        return {}
    rows = session.execute(
        select(Result.run_id, Result.status, func.count())
        .where(Result.run_id.in_(run_ids))
        .group_by(Result.run_id, Result.status)
    ).all()
    by_run: dict[uuid.UUID, dict[str, int]] = defaultdict(dict)
    for run_id, status, n in rows:
        by_run[run_id][status] = n
    out: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]] = {}
    for run_id, by_status in by_run.items():
        passed = by_status.get("pass", 0)
        # Worst check outcome via the single shared severity helper (#655); skip/error
        # aren't failing tiers, so they never rank.
        worst = worst_severity(by_status)
        # Evaluated checks only: pass + the three failing tiers (skip/error excluded).
        total = passed + sum(by_status.get(tier, 0) for tier in SEVERITY_RANK)
        out[run_id] = (total, passed, worst)
    return out


def get_run(session: Session, run_id: uuid.UUID) -> Run | None:
    """Fetch a run by id (no authz — the API layer gates on the run's suite)."""
    return session.get(Run, run_id)


_TERMINAL_STATUSES = frozenset({"succeeded", "failed", "cancelled"})
# The complement, single-sourced from the canonical status set so a new
# lifecycle status can't silently escape the reaper's net (#309).
_NON_TERMINAL_STATUSES = frozenset(RUN_STATUSES) - _TERMINAL_STATUSES

content.text: Now I understand the vocab: run `status` (queued/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled) vs result `status` (severity tiers + operational `skip`/`error`). A run can be `succeeded` while individual checks `error` — so `has_failed_run` alone misses per-check operational errors. To honor "error/skip results feed connection health," I need error/skip presence on the summary. Let me map the full consumer surface.

=== run.status set to failed vs per-check error ===
        # was reaped (failed + REAPED_REASON) can still finish and commit success

        run.failure_reason = None
        session.commit()
    except Exception as exc:
        session.rollback()

        # mid-flight that *also* errored stays 'cancelled', not masked as 'failed'.
        if _cancelled_mid_run(session, run):
            log.info("run_cancelled_during_execution", run_id=str(run.id))
            return run
        run.status = "failed"
        run.finished_at = _now()
        # Redaction-safe reason (#605): classify the exception into a fixed
        # message — the raw text (which can carry DSN/credential fragments) stays
        # in the server log below, never on the persisted/surfaced reason.
        run.failure_reason = classify_failure_reason(exc)
        session.commit()
        log.exception("run_failed", run_id=str(run.id), table=table)
        return run

    log.info(
        "run_completed",
        run_id=str(run.id),
        suite_success=all(o.success for o in outcomes),
        n_results=len(rows),
    )
    return run


def skip_run(session: Session, *, run: Run, checks: list[Check], reason: str) -> Run:
    """Record a run that had nothing to evaluate — every check `skip`ped (#122).

    Used when the adapter is never invoked because there's no data to validate
    (e.g. the target batch hasn't landed yet). The run still **succeeds** — it
    executed end to end, it just found nothing to check — and each check gets a
    ``skip`` Result carrying the ``reason`` (operational, not a severity tier, so
    it's excluded from the health-score N per ADR 0005). Distinct from ``failed``,
    which means the run could not execute.
    """
    run.status = "running"
    run.started_at = _now()
    session.commit()
    rows = [
        Result(run_id=run.id, check_id=check.id, status="skip", observed_value={"reason": reason})
        for check in checks
    ]
    session.add_all(rows)
    run.status = "succeeded"
    run.finished_at = _now()
    session.commit()

        # message — the raw text (which can carry DSN/credential fragments) stays
        # in the server log below, never on the persisted/surfaced reason.
        run.failure_reason = classify_failure_reason(exc)
        session.commit()
        log.exception("run_failed", run_id=str(run.id), table=table)
        return run

    log.info(
        "run_completed",
        run_id=str(run.id),
        suite_success=all(o.success for o in outcomes),
        n_results=len(rows),
    )
    return run


def skip_run(session: Session, *, run: Run, checks: list[Check], reason: str) -> Run:
    """Record a run that had nothing to evaluate — every check `skip`ped (#122).

    Used when the adapter is never invoked because there's no data to validate
    (e.g. the target batch hasn't landed yet). The run still **succeeds** — it
    executed end to end, it just found nothing to check — and each check gets a
    ``skip`` Result carrying the ``reason`` (operational, not a severity tier, so
    it's excluded from the health-score N per ADR 0005). Distinct from ``failed``,
    which means the run could not execute.
    """
    run.status = "running"
    run.started_at = _now()
    session.commit()
    rows = [
        Result(run_id=run.id, check_id=check.id, status="skip", observed_value={"reason": reason})
        for check in checks
    ]
    session.add_all(rows)
    run.status = "succeeded"
    run.finished_at = _now()
    session.commit()

from backend.app.db.session import get_db
from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc

router = APIRouter(tags=["assets"])


class RunOutcomeRead(ApiModel):
    """A suite's latest run outcome — execution status + the DQ summary."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    run_id: uuid.UUID | None
    status: str | None
    worst_severity: str | None
    checks_total: int
    checks_passed: int
    finished_at: datetime | None
    created_at: datetime | None


class ComposingSuiteRead(ApiModel):
    """One suite the caller can see that targets the asset, with its latest run."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    suite_id: uuid.UUID
    name: str
    my_permission: str
    latest_run: RunOutcomeRead


class AssetSummaryRead(ApiModel):
    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset. `worst_severity` /
    `checks_*` / `last_run_at` roll up the caller-visible composing suites' latest
    runs; `worst_severity` is null when all passed or nothing has run."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    name: str
    env: str | None
    description: str | None
    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
    last_seen: datetime
    suite_count: int
    worst_severity: str | None
    checks_total: int
    checks_passed: int
    last_run_at: datetime | None

    # suite's latest run `failed` / still `queued`/`running`. The UI health badge
    # reads these so an operationally-failed run never renders green.
    has_failed_run: bool

RUN_STATUSES = ("queued", "running", "succeeded", "failed", "cancelled")
# Result statuses. The four severity tiers (ADR 0005) are health-score-bearing —
# the score aggregate sums their weights over N. The two operational statuses
# (#122) are orthogonal: 'skip' = not evaluated, 'error' = evaluation threw
# (distinct from 'fail', a successful evaluation that breached). Operational
# statuses carry NO penalty weight and MUST be excluded from the health-score N
# (i.e. aggregate WHERE status IN the four tiers only).

_RESULT_OPERATIONAL_STATUSES = ("skip", "error")

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunOutcome:
    """A suite's latest run outcome — execution status + the data-quality summary.

    ``worst_severity`` is the highest failing tier among evaluated checks (warn <
    fail < critical), or ``None`` when all passed / nothing evaluated. ``run_id`` /
    timestamps are ``None`` when the suite has never run.

    ``has_error`` / ``has_skip`` are the run's **operational** results (#122) — a
    check the datasource threw on, or one whose precondition wasn't met. They are
    deliberately *not* severity: they say nothing about data quality, only about
    whether DataQ could evaluate at all, and so feed connection health (#803)."""

    run_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
    status: str | None = None
    worst_severity: str | None = None
    checks_total: int = 0
    checks_passed: int = 0
    finished_at: datetime | None = None
    created_at: datetime | None = None

input.new_string:
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AssetSummary:
    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset.

    **Two orthogonal health axes (#803).** The old single "health" conflated them:

    - *Suite health* (data quality, ADR 0005) — ``worst_severity`` over the
      **evaluated** checks, plus ``checks_total``/``checks_passed``. Operational
      results never rank here, so a datasource DataQ couldn't reach reads as "no
      data", never as a green "passing" nor as a red data failure.
    - *Connection health* (reachability) — ``has_operational_error`` /
      ``has_skip``: could DataQ execute against the datasource at all? Derived
      **purely from the runs already recorded** (a latest run that `failed`, or
      any ``error``/``skip`` result on one) — there is no connection-probe polling
      loop behind this, by design.
    """

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    name: str
    env: str | None
    description: str | None
    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
    last_seen: datetime
    suite_count: int
    # ── suite health (data quality) ──
    worst_severity: str | None
    checks_total: int
    checks_passed: int
    last_run_at: datetime | None
    # ── connection health (reachability / execution) ──
    # `has_failed_run`: any latest run whose *execution* `failed` (wrote no results).
    # `has_active_run`: any latest run still `queued`/`running` (hasn't concluded).
    # `has_operational_error`: a failed run OR any `error` result — DataQ could not
    #   evaluate against the datasource. `has_skip`: any `skip` result (a
    #   precondition, e.g. the batch hasn't landed, wasn't met) — degraded, not down.
    has_failed_run: bool = False
    has_active_run: bool = False
    has_operational_error: bool = False
    has_skip: bool = False

input.old_string:
def _run_outcome(run: Run | None, outcome: tuple[int, int, str | None] | None) -> RunOutcome:
    """Assemble a `RunOutcome` from a suite's latest run + its check-outcome tuple."""
    if run is None:
        return RunOutcome()
    total, passed, worst = outcome or (0, 0, None)
    return RunOutcome(
        run_id=run.id,
        status=run.status,
        worst_severity=worst,
        checks_total=total,
        checks_passed=passed,
        finished_at=run.finished_at,
        created_at=run.created_at,
    )


def _composing_suites(
    suites: list[Suite],
    levels: dict[uuid.UUID, str | None],
    latest_runs: dict[uuid.UUID, Run],
    outcomes: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]],
) -> list[ComposingSuite]:
    """Build the per-suite breakdown for one asset's suites (sorted by name)."""
    composing: list[ComposingSuite] = []
    for suite in suites:
        level = levels.get(suite.id)
        if level is None:  # defensive: only reachable suites are passed in
            continue
        run = latest_runs.get(suite.id)
        outcome = outcomes.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
        composing.append(
            ComposingSuite(
                suite_id=suite.id,
                name=suite.name,
                my_permission=level,
                latest_run=_run_outcome(run, outcome),
            )
        )
    return composing
input.new_string:
def _run_outcome(
    run: Run | None,
    outcome: tuple[int, int, str | None] | None,
    op_flags: tuple[bool, bool] | None = None,
) -> RunOutcome:
    """Assemble a `RunOutcome` from a suite's latest run + its check-outcome tuple
    + its operational (`error`/`skip`) flags."""
    if run is None:
        return RunOutcome()
    total, passed, worst = outcome or (0, 0, None)
    has_error, has_skip = op_flags or (False, False)
    return RunOutcome(
        run_id=run.id,
        status=run.status,
        worst_severity=worst,
        checks_total=total,
        checks_passed=passed,
        finished_at=run.finished_at,
        created_at=run.created_at,
        has_error=has_error,
        has_skip=has_skip,
    )


def _composing_suites(
    suites: list[Suite],
    levels: dict[uuid.UUID, str | None],
    latest_runs: dict[uuid.UUID, Run],
    outcomes: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]],
    op_flags: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[bool, bool]] | None = None,
) -> list[ComposingSuite]:
    """Build the per-suite breakdown for one asset's suites (sorted by name)."""
    op_flags = op_flags or {}
    composing: list[ComposingSuite] = []
    for suite in suites:
        level = levels.get(suite.id)
        if level is None:  # defensive: only reachable suites are passed in
            continue
        run = latest_runs.get(suite.id)
        outcome = outcomes.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
        flags = op_flags.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
        composing.append(
            ComposingSuite(
                suite_id=suite.id,
                name=suite.name,
                my_permission=level,
                latest_run=_run_outcome(run, outcome, flags),
            )
        )
    return composing

# get_visible_asset + summarize_asset share this exact 3-line shape

    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)""",
)
p.write_text(s)

1	"""Read-only asset view — the browse/reason surface over `assets` (ADR 0034, #760).
2	
3	Assets are what users *reason about*; suites remain how checks *execute* (ADR 0034
4	guiding principle). This module aggregates, per asset, the suites that target it +
5	their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood, for the `/assets` API.
6	
7	**Authz is derived, never granted (ADR 0034 decision 5 / ADR 0027).** An asset is
8	visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 suite mapped to it (`suites.asset_id`); the
9	aggregation is filtered to *only* the suites the caller's grants cover; a
10	workspace-admin sees every suite (`include_all`). An asset wholly outside the
11	caller's grants is 404-no-leak (the API layer raises `AssetNotFoundError`). This
12	reuses `suite_service.accessible_suite_ids` verbatim, so the visibility rule has a
13	single source of truth and can never drift from the suites/runs surfaces.
14	
15	Asset-metadata mutation (owner, description) is workspace-Admin-only — enforced at
16	the API layer (`require_workspace_admin`), not here; `update_asset_metadata` is the
17	plain persistence half.
18	
19	Lineage nodes are **not** authz-filtered: blast radius is the point (ADR 0034 §2),
20	and a node exposes only its OpenLineage `(namespace, name)` + whether it is
21	monitored — never run data or grants — so nothing sensitive leaks through it.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import uuid
27	from collections import defaultdict
28	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
29	from datetime import datetime
30	
31	from sqlalchemy import select
32	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
33	
34	from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
35	from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
36	from backend.app.db.models import Asset, Run, Suite, User, worst_severity
37	from backend.app.lineage.edges import downstream_assets, upstream_assets
38	from backend.app.services.run_service import check_outcome_counts, operational_result_flags
39	from backend.app.services.suite_authz import effective_permissions
40	from backend.app.services.suite_service import accessible_suite_ids
41	
42	log = get_logger(__name__)
43	
44	
45	class AssetNotFoundError(DataQError):
46	    """Raised when an asset does not exist *or* is wholly outside the caller's
47	    grants — the two are indistinguishable by design (404-no-leak, ADR 0027)."""
48	
49	    status_code = 404
50	    code = "asset_not_found"
51	
52	
53	class AssetOwnerInvalidError(DataQError):
54	    """The `owner_user_id` on a metadata update names no existing user — checked
55	    up front (the share-grant FK-precheck idiom, `share_service.grant_share`) so a
56	    bad id is a clean 422, never a raw FK IntegrityError surfacing as 500."""
57	
58	    status_code = 422
59	    code = "asset_owner_invalid"
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True)
63	class RunOutcome:
64	    """A suite's latest run outcome — execution status + the data-quality summary.
65	
66	    ``worst_severity`` is the highest failing tier among evaluated checks (warn <
67	    fail < critical), or ``None`` when all passed / nothing evaluated. ``run_id`` /
68	    timestamps are ``None`` when the suite has never run.
69	
70	    ``has_error`` / ``has_skip`` are the run's **operational** results (#122) — a
71	    check the datasource threw on, or one whose precondition wasn't met. They are
72	    deliberately *not* severity: they say nothing about data quality, only about
73	    whether DataQ could evaluate at all, and so feed connection health (#803)."""
74	
75	    run_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
76	    status: str | None = None
77	    worst_severity: str | None = None
78	    checks_total: int = 0
79	    checks_passed: int = 0
80	    finished_at: datetime | None = None
81	    created_at: datetime | None = None
82	    has_error: bool = False
83	    has_skip: bool = False
84	
85	
86	@dataclass(frozen=True)
87	class ComposingSuite:
88	    """One suite the caller can see that targets the asset, with its latest run."""
89	
90	    suite_id: uuid.UUID
91	    name: str
92	    my_permission: str
93	    latest_run: RunOutcome
94	
95	
96	@dataclass(frozen=True)
97	class AssetSummary:
98	    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset.
99	
100	    **Two orthogonal health axes (#803).** The old single "health" conflated them:
101	
102	    - *Suite health* (data quality, ADR 0005) — ``worst_severity`` over the
103	      **evaluated** checks, plus ``checks_total``/``checks_passed``. Operational
104	      results never rank here, so a datasource DataQ couldn't reach reads as "no
105	      data", never as a green "passing" nor as a red data failure.
106	    - *Connection health* (reachability) — ``has_operational_error`` /
107	      ``has_skip``: could DataQ execute against the datasource at all? Derived
108	      **purely from the runs already recorded** (a latest run that `failed`, or
109	      any ``error``/``skip`` result on one) — there is no connection-probe polling
110	      loop behind this, by design.
111	    """
112	
113	    id: uuid.UUID
114	    namespace: str
115	    name: str
116	    env: str | None
117	    description: str | None
118	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
119	    last_seen: datetime
120	    suite_count: int
121	    # ── suite health (data quality) ──
122	    worst_severity: str | None
123	    checks_total: int
124	    checks_passed: int
125	    last_run_at: datetime | None
126	    # ── connection health (reachability / execution) ──
127	    # `has_failed_run`: any latest run whose *execution* `failed` (wrote no results).
128	    # `has_active_run`: any latest run still `queued`/`running` (hasn't concluded).
129	    # `has_operational_error`: a failed run OR any `error` result — DataQ could not
130	    #   evaluate against the datasource. `has_skip`: any `skip` result (a
131	    #   precondition, e.g. the batch hasn't landed, wasn't met) — degraded, not down.
132	    has_failed_run: bool = False
133	    has_active_run: bool = False
134	    has_operational_error: bool = False
135	    has_skip: bool = False
136	
137	
138	@dataclass(frozen=True)
139	class LineageNode:
140	    """A lineage neighbour — enough to render, no run data (ADR 0034 §2)."""
141	
142	    id: uuid.UUID
143	    namespace: str
144	    name: str
145	    env: str | None
146	    is_monitored: bool
147	
148	
149	@dataclass(frozen=True)
150	class AssetDetail:
151	    """Asset detail: the summary aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage."""
152	
153	    summary: AssetSummary
154	    suites: list[ComposingSuite]
155	    upstream: list[LineageNode] = field(default_factory=list)
156	    downstream: list[LineageNode] = field(default_factory=list)
157	
158	
159	# ── internals ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
160	
161	
162	def _latest_run_per_suite(session: Session, suite_ids: list[uuid.UUID]) -> dict[uuid.UUID, Run]:
163	    """The most-recent run for each suite (DISTINCT ON, newest `created_at`)."""
164	    if not suite_ids:
165	        return {}
166	    rows = session.scalars(
167	        select(Run)
168	        .where(Run.suite_id.in_(suite_ids))
169	        .distinct(Run.suite_id)
170	        .order_by(Run.suite_id, Run.created_at.desc())
171	    )
172	    return {run.suite_id: run for run in rows}
173	
174	
175	def _run_outcome(
176	    run: Run | None,
177	    outcome: tuple[int, int, str | None] | None,
178	    op_flags: tuple[bool, bool] | None = None,
179	) -> RunOutcome:
180	    """Assemble a `RunOutcome` from a suite's latest run + its check-outcome tuple
181	    + its operational (`error`/`skip`) flags."""
182	    if run is None:
183	        return RunOutcome()
184	    total, passed, worst = outcome or (0, 0, None)
185	    has_error, has_skip = op_flags or (False, False)
186	    return RunOutcome(
187	        run_id=run.id,
188	        status=run.status,
189	        worst_severity=worst,
190	        checks_total=total,
191	        checks_passed=passed,
192	        finished_at=run.finished_at,
193	        created_at=run.created_at,
194	        has_error=has_error,
195	        has_skip=has_skip,
196	    )
197	
198	
199	def _composing_suites(
200	    suites: list[Suite],
201	    levels: dict[uuid.UUID, str | None],

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Read-only asset view API (ADR 0034, gap G-d phase 2, #760).

Assets are the browse/reason grain over the suite execution grain. This surface
lists the assets a caller can see and drills into one — its composing suites +
their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood.

**Authz is derived, never granted (ADR 0027 / ADR 0034 decision 5):** an asset is
visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 suite targeting it; the aggregation is
filtered to the caller's grants; a workspace-admin sees all; an asset wholly
outside the caller's grants is 404-no-leak. All of that lives in
`asset_view_service` (which reuses the suites/runs visibility subquery), so this
module is a thin HTTP layer.

Asset-metadata mutation (`PATCH`) is **workspace-Admin-only** (ADR 0034 §4) —
gated by `require_workspace_admin`, the same 403 the /admin surface uses.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query
from pydantic import ConfigDict, Field
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from backend.app.api.v1._base import ApiModel
from backend.app.core.auth import get_current_user, is_workspace_admin, require_workspace_admin
from backend.app.db.models import User
from backend.app.db.session import get_db
from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc

router = APIRouter(tags=["assets"])


class RunOutcomeRead(ApiModel):
    """A suite's latest run outcome — execution status + the DQ summary."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    run_id: uuid.UUID | None
    status: str | None
    worst_severity: str | None
    checks_total: int
    checks_passed: int
    finished_at: datetime | None
    created_at: datetime | None


class ComposingSuiteRead(ApiModel):
    """One suite the caller can see that targets the asset, with its latest run."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    suite_id: uuid.UUID
    name: str
    my_permission: str
    latest_run: RunOutcomeRead


class AssetSummaryRead(ApiModel):
    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset. `worst_severity` /
    `checks_*` / `last_run_at` roll up the caller-visible composing suites' latest
    runs; `worst_severity` is null when all passed or nothing has run."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    name: str
    env: str | None
    description: str | None
    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
    last_seen: datetime
    suite_count: int
    worst_severity: str | None
    checks_total: int
    checks_passed: int
    last_run_at: datetime | None
    # Latest-run execution states (distinct from check severity): any composing
    # suite's latest run `failed` / still `queued`/`running`. The UI health badge
    # reads these so an operationally-failed run never renders green.
    has_failed_run: bool
    has_active_run: bool


class LineageNodeRead(ApiModel):
    """A lineage neighbour — OpenLineage identity + whether it is monitored. No
    run data (blast-radius browse only; ADR 0034 §2)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    name: str
    env: str | None
    is_monitored: bool


class AssetDetailRead(ApiModel):
    """Asset detail: the summary + per-suite breakdown + upstream/downstream lineage."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    summary: AssetSummaryRead
    suites: list[ComposingSuiteRead]
    upstream: list[LineageNodeRead]
    downstream: list[LineageNodeRead]


class AssetMetadataUpdate(ApiModel):
    """Partial metadata update (workspace-Admin-only). Each field is optional; an
    explicit `null` clears it, an omitted field leaves it unchanged — the two are
    distinguished via `model_fields_set` at the route so `owner_user_id: null`
    means "unassign" rather than "leave as is".

    `extra="forbid"`: because omitted-vs-null is semantically load-bearing here, a
    typo'd field name (`descripton`) must be a 422, not a silently-ignored no-op."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
    # Same cap as suite descriptions (SuiteCreate).
    description: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=1024)


_LIST_LIMIT_DEFAULT = 200
_LIST_LIMIT_MAX = 200


@router.get("/assets", response_model=list[AssetSummaryRead], summary="List visible assets")
def list_assets(
    current_user: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
    limit: int = Query(default=_LIST_LIMIT_DEFAULT, ge=1, le=_LIST_LIMIT_MAX),
    offset: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
) -> list[svc.AssetSummary]:
    # Visibility derived from suite grants — a workspace-admin sees every asset
    # (ADR 0027), everyone else only assets with a suite they can view. Pages are
    # a stable (namespace, name) ordering sliced by limit/offset.
    return svc.list_visible_assets(
        db,
        user_id=current_user.id,
        include_all=is_workspace_admin(current_user),
        limit=limit,
        offset=offset,
    )


@router.get("/assets/{asset_id}", response_model=AssetDetailRead, summary="Get an asset")
def get_asset(
    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
    current_user: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> svc.AssetDetail:
    # Raises AssetNotFoundError (404) for an unknown asset OR one the caller can
    # see no composing suite for — existence hidden (no-leak, ADR 0027).
    return svc.get_visible_asset(
        db, asset_id, user_id=current_user.id, include_all=is_workspace_admin(current_user)
    )


@router.patch(
    "/assets/{asset_id}",
    response_model=AssetSummaryRead,
    summary="Update asset metadata (workspace-admin only)",
)
def update_asset(
    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
    payload: AssetMetadataUpdate,
    # Workspace-Admin-only (ADR 0034 §4) — a non-admin gets a real 403.
    _admin: Annotated[User, Depends(require_workspace_admin)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> svc.AssetSummary:
    fields = payload.model_fields_set
    asset = svc.update_asset_metadata(
        db,
        asset_id,
        owner_user_id=payload.owner_user_id,
        description=payload.description,
        set_owner="owner_user_id" in fields,
        set_description="description" in fields,
    )
    # Return the refreshed summary (admin sees all → include_all). Never 404s on
    # an asset with no composing suites — metadata exists independently of suites.
    return svc.summarize_asset(db, asset, user_id=_admin.id, include_all=True)

lines: -    `checks_*` / `last_run_at` roll up the caller-visible composing suites' latest

-    # suite's latest run `failed` / still `queued`/`running`. The UI health badge
-    # reads these so an operationally-failed run never renders green.
+    # Connection health (#803): a failed run OR any `error` result → DataQ could not
+    # evaluate against the datasource; `skip` → a precondition wasn't met (degraded).

file.content:
"""Service-level tests for `asset_view_service` — the branches the HTTP authz
matrix (tests/api/test_assets.py) doesn't reach: metadata partial-update
semantics, an asset with no composing suites, and the empty-input short-circuits.

Skips without TEST_DATABASE_URL (JSONB/UUID need real Postgres)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any

import pytest

from backend.app.db.models import Asset, Connection, User
from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc
from backend.app.services import suite_service


def _user(db: Any) -> User:
    u = User(aad_object_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, email=f"{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}@ex.com")
    db.add(u)
    db.flush()
    return u


def _conn(db: Any, owner: User) -> Connection:
    c = Connection(
        name=f"sf-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        type="snowflake",
        env="dev",
        config={"account": "ab12345.eu-west-1", "database": "ANALYTICS", "schema": "PUBLIC"},
        secret_ref="kv-x",
        created_by=owner.id,
    )
    db.add(c)
    db.commit()
    return c


def test_list_empty_when_no_visible_suites(db_session: Any) -> None:
    user = _user(db_session)
    assert svc.list_visible_assets(db_session, user_id=user.id) == []


def test_summarize_asset_with_no_suites(db_session: Any) -> None:
    """An orphan asset (e.g. a dbt-lineage-only node) summarizes to an empty,
    no-run health — never raises, so the admin PATCH response works on it."""
    asset = Asset(namespace="snowflake://x", name="ORPHAN")
    db_session.add(asset)
    db_session.commit()
    admin = _user(db_session)
    summary = svc.summarize_asset(db_session, asset, user_id=admin.id, include_all=True)
    assert summary.suite_count == 0
    assert summary.worst_severity is None
    assert summary.last_run_at is None
    assert summary.checks_total == 0


def test_update_metadata_partial_leaves_untouched(db_session: Any) -> None:
    owner = _user(db_session)
    conn = _conn(db_session, owner)
    suite = suite_service.create_suite(
        db_session,
        name="S",
        description=None,
        connection_id=conn.id,
        created_by=owner.id,
        target={"table": "ORDERS"},
    )
    asset_id = suite.asset_id
    assert asset_id is not None

    # Set description only — owner stays NULL (set_owner=False).
    svc.update_asset_metadata(db_session, asset_id, description="v1", set_description=True)
    asset = db_session.get(Asset, asset_id)
    assert asset.description == "v1"
    assert asset.owner_user_id is None

    # Set owner only — description untouched (still v1).
    svc.update_asset_metadata(db_session, asset_id, owner_user_id=owner.id, set_owner=True)
    db_session.refresh(asset)
    assert asset.owner_user_id == owner.id
    assert asset.description == "v1"

    # Explicit clear of description to None (set_description=True, value None).
    svc.update_asset_metadata(db_session, asset_id, description=None, set_description=True)
    db_session.refresh(asset)
    assert asset.description is None


def test_update_metadata_unknown_raises(db_session: Any) -> None:
    with pytest.raises(svc.AssetNotFoundError):
        svc.update_asset_metadata(db_session, uuid.uuid4(), description="x", set_description=True)


def test_get_unknown_asset_raises(db_session: Any) -> None:
    user = _user(db_session)
    with pytest.raises(svc.AssetNotFoundError):
        svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, uuid.uuid4(), user_id=user.id)

def test_get_unknown_asset_raises(db_session: Any) -> None:
    user = _user(db_session)
    with pytest.raises(svc.AssetNotFoundError):
        svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, uuid.uuid4(), user_id=user.id)


# ── connection health vs suite health (#803) ─────────────────────────────────
#
# The two axes must not bleed into each other: operational `error`/`skip` results
# (#122) feed *connection* health (could DataQ reach the datasource?) and are
# invisible to *suite* health (is the data good?), which is severity-only.


def _suite_with_run(db: Any, owner: User, *, run_status: str, result_statuses: list[str]) -> Asset:
    """A suite on a fresh asset with one run carrying `result_statuses` results."""
    conn = _conn(db, owner)
    suite = suite_service.create_suite(
        db,
        name=f"S-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
        description=None,
        connection_id=conn.id,
        created_by=owner.id,
        target={"table": f"T{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"},
    )
    run = Run(suite_id=suite.id, status=run_status, triggered_by="manual")
    db.add(run)
    db.flush()
    for status in result_statuses:
        check = Check(
            suite_id=suite.id,
            name=f"c-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
            expectation_type="expect_column_to_exist",
            kwargs={"column": "X"},
        )
        db.add(check)
        db.flush()
        db.add(Result(run_id=run.id, check_id=check.id, status=status))
    db.commit()
    assert suite.asset_id is not None
    return db.get(Asset, suite.asset_id)


def test_error_result_feeds_connection_health_not_suite_health(db_session: Any) -> None:
    """A run that SUCCEEDED but whose check threw: connection health is degraded
    (operational error) while suite health stays severity-free — the exact case
    `has_failed_run` alone misses, since the run itself never failed."""
    owner = _user(db_session)
    asset = _suite_with_run(db_session, owner, run_status="succeeded", result_statuses=["error"])
    s = svc.summarize_asset(db_session, asset, user_id=owner.id, include_all=True)

    assert s.has_operational_error is True  # connection axis: could not evaluate
    assert s.has_failed_run is False  # the run itself succeeded
    assert s.worst_severity is None  # suite axis: no DQ verdict at all
    assert s.checks_total == 0  # `error` is not an evaluated check


def test_skip_result_is_degraded_not_an_error(db_session: Any) -> None:
    """`skip` = a precondition wasn't met (the batch hasn't landed). The run
    executed, so it is NOT an operational error — only a degraded connection."""
    owner = _user(db_session)
    asset = _suite_with_run(db_session, owner, run_status="succeeded", result_statuses=["skip"])
    s = svc.summarize_asset(db_session, asset, user_id=owner.id, include_all=True)

    assert s.has_skip is True
    assert s.has_operational_error is False
    assert s.worst_severity is None
    assert s.checks_total == 0


def test_failed_run_is_an_operational_error(db_session: Any) -> None:
    """A run whose execution failed wrote no results at all — connection axis."""
    owner = _user(db_session)
    asset = _suite_with_run(db_session, owner, run_status="failed", result_statuses=[])
    s = svc.summarize_asset(db_session, asset, user_id=owner.id, include_all=True)

    assert s.has_failed_run is True
    assert s.has_operational_error is True
    assert s.worst_severity is None


def test_failing_data_does_not_touch_connection_health(db_session: Any) -> None:
    """The mirror case: the datasource was perfectly reachable, the DATA is bad.
    Suite health goes red; connection health stays clean."""
    owner = _user(db_session)
    asset = _suite_with_run(
        db_session, owner, run_status="succeeded", result_statuses=["pass", "critical"]
    )
    s = svc.summarize_asset(db_session, asset, user_id=owner.id, include_all=True)

    assert s.worst_severity == "critical"  # suite axis: data is bad
    assert s.checks_total == 2 and s.checks_passed == 1
    assert s.has_operational_error is False  # connection axis: nothing wrong here
    assert s.has_skip is False


def test_operational_result_flags_empty_input() -> None:
    assert run_service.operational_result_flags(None, []) == {}  # type: ignore[arg-type]

matrix (tests/api/test_assets.py) doesn't reach: metadata partial-update
semantics, an asset with no composing suites, and the empty-input short-circuits.

Skips without TEST_DATABASE_URL (JSONB/UUID need real Postgres)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any

import pytest

from backend.app.db.models import Asset, Connection, User
from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc
from backend.app.services import suite_service


def _user(db: Any) -> User:
    u = User(aad_object_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, email=f"{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}@ex.com")
    db.add(u)
    db.flush()
    return u


def _conn(db: Any, owner: User) -> Connection:
    c = Connection(
        name=f"sf-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        type="snowflake",
        env="dev",
